mappings.rationale: Directly covers plant tissues (meristematic, dermal, vascular, ground), plant organs (stem, leaf, root), and diverse forms of shoot and root systems (B and C).
Covers animal tissues, organ systems, and body plans across invertebrate and vertebrate phyla (D and E), including muscle, nervous, and reproductive systems.
Addresses vertebrate organ systems (E.1) including skeletal, muscular, nervous, circulatory, digestive, and respiratory systems, plus their interrelationships.
Covers the functional aspects of organ systems and feedback mechanisms (E.2), illustrating the relation of form and function in the human body.
Provides the cellular basis for understanding how cells organize into tissues (B.3, D.6), the building blocks of organs and organ systems.
Addresses development of organ systems (E.3) and evolution of plant and animal organs into complex multicellular states (C.5).
Covers blood as an animal tissue and fluid (D.2) including its roles in transport and its classification among circulating tissues.
Relevant to endocrine tissues (D.3) and the reproductive system (E.1.c), covering hormonal coordination among organ systems.
Illustrates the relationship between form and function in a specific organ, covering skeletal and connective tissue organization (D.4).
Covers a specialized sensory organ illustrating nervous and sensory tissue integration (D.3), and organ development and evolution.
Illustrates vertebrate organ systems and diversity of form and function across mammalian body plans (E).
Relevant to biological form and function (A) through microbiome interactions that influence organ function and tissue organization in host organisms.
Covers the relatively undifferentiated tissues of nonvascular organisms (B.1), including fungal cell walls and tissue organization.
